1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

11	/-!
12	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
13	
14	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
15	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
16	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
17	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
18	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
19	
20	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
21	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
22	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
23	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
24	
25	1. a binary tensor-product operation
26	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
27	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
28	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
29	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
30	
31	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
32	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
33	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
34	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
35	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
36	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
37	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
38	
39	## Status (current)
40	
41	`tensorObj` and `tensorObj_functoriality` are fully defined (no `sorry`), lifting
42	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification on the small Zariski
43	site. The remaining typed-`sorry` residuals are the `⊗`-inverse lane
44	(`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`) and the route-(e)
45	whiskering residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. The dual-block is now
46	complete axiom-clean: the value layer (`InternalHom.internalHom`, `dual`,
47	`evalLin`/`internalHomEvalApp`) plus the evaluation morphism `internalHomEval`
48	(its naturality CLOSED iter-224, see below). Once the inverse lands, the consumer
49	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` closes the residual `addCommGroup`
50	sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` L235.
51	
52	iter-224 on `internalHomEval`'s naturality: CLOSED axiom-clean. The iter-222/223 `whnf`
53	heartbeat-bomb diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit
54	machinery on the first rewrite) was STALE — a Mathlib update made the composition split cleanly
55	with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]`, after which the six-step `evalLin`/`naturality_apply`/`hdt`
56	reduction goes through with no bomb, no `with_reducible`, and no `maxHeartbeats` bump.
57	
58	The 3 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
59	
60	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) — the substrate binary
61	   operation `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`,
62	   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
63	   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.
64	   Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`.

126	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
127	
128	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
129	
130	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
131	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
132	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
133	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
134	
135	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
136	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
137	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
138	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
139	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
140	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
141	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
142	
143	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
144	
145	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
146	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
147	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
148	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
149	
150	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
151	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
152	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
153	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
154	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
155	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
156	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
157	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
158	
159	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
160	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
161	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
162	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
163	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
164	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
165	law is definitional. -/
166	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
167	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
168	
169	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
170	
171	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
172	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
173	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
174	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
175	
176	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
177	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
178	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
179	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
180	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
181	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
182	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
183	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
184	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
185	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
186	
187	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
188	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
189	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
190	
191	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
192	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
193	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
194	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
195	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
196	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
197	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
198	
199	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
200	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
201	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
202	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
203	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
204	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
205	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
206	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
207	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
208	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
209	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
210	
211	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
212	
213	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
214	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
215	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
216	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
217	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
218	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
219	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
220	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
221	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
222	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
223	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
224	
225	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
226	
227	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
228	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
229	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
230	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
231	
232	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
233	
234	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
235	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
236	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
237	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
238	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
239	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
240	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
241	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
242	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
243	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
244	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
245	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
246	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
247	
248	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
249	sheaf.**
250	
251	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
252	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
253	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
254	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
255	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
256	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
257	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
258	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
259	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
260	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
261	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
262	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
263	
264	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
265	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
266	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
267	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
268	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
269	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
270	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
271	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
272	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
273	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
274	
275	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
276	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
277	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
278	sheafification counit. -/
279	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
280	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
281	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
282	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
283	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
284	
285	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
286	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
287	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
288	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
289	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
290	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
291	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
292	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
293	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
294	
295	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X` on `⊗`-invertible objects.** (Blueprint
296	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
297	For `M, N, P` `⊗`-invertible (hence locally free of rank one, hence sectionwise
298	flat), there is an isomorphism `(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the
299	objectwise existence-of-iso datum the group law
300	`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
301	
302	Status: CLOSED at the direct-`sorry` level (no `sorry` in this declaration's body);
303	it is `sorry`-transitive only through the route-(e) residual
304	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. iter-212 go/no-go bridge CLEARED, the residual
305	located. The construction is the blueprint's three-step composite. Writing
306	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)`,
307	`η = sheafificationAdjunction.unit` (the localizer unit, `toPresheaf`-image
308	`toSheafify ∈ J.W`), and `α` the presheaf-of-modules associator:
309	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso  (P flat ⇒ right-whiskered `η ∈ J.W`
310	     by `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`; `a` inverts `J.W` by `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`),
311	     giving `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P = a(a(M.val⊗N.val).val ⊗ P.val) ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
312	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`;
313	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso  (M flat), giving
314	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ a(M.val ⊗ a(N.val⊗P.val).val) = M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
315	
316	**The go/no-go bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)` is now
317	CLOSED, axiom-clean**, as `PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (it is
318	`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` — the
319	sheafification functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` —
320	read at one morphism). The right-whisker conjugate
321	`PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat` is also closed axiom-clean.
322	
323	**The genuine residual is now the flatness feeding steps 1 and 3**: steps 1/3
324	need `J.W (toPresheaf (η ▷ P.val))` / `J.W (toPresheaf (M.val ◁ η))`, which
325	`W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` supply ONLY from the SECTIONWISE flatness instance
326	`∀ U : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val(U))` (resp. M). This is NOT
327	derivable from `IsInvertible`: the only Mathlib route is single-ring
328	`Module.Invertible R m → Projective → Flat`, which would require `P.val(U)` to be
329	an invertible `𝒪_X(U)`-module for EVERY open `U` — false in general (the global
330	sections functor over a non-affine open is not exact and does not preserve
331	flatness; invertibility is a LOCAL/affine property). The blueprint's
332	"invertible ⇒ projective ⇒ flat" conflates global module-invertibility with
333	sectionwise-over-all-opens flatness. The mathematically-correct fix scopes
334	injectivity LOCALLY (local-triviality whiskering: on the cover where `P ≅ 𝒪`,
335	`η ▷ P ≅ η`, locally injective) — a new lemma needing `IsInvertible ⇒
336	IsLocallyTrivial` (blueprint-flagged nontrivial). A second obstacle is pure Lean

340	heartbeat timeouts. See task result for the full corrected decomposition. -/
341	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
342	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
343	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
344	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
345	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
346	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
347	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
348	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
349	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
350	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
351	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
352	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
353	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
354	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
355	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
356	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
357	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
358	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
359	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
360	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
361	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
362	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
363	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
364	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
365	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
366	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
367	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _

369	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
370	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
371	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
372	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
373	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
374	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
375	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
376	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
377	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
378	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3

381	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)

384	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
385	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
386	
387	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
388	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
389	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
390	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
391	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
392	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
393	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
394	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
395	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
396	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
397	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
398	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
399	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
400	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
401	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
402	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
403	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
404	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
405	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
406	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
407	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
408	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
409	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
410	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
411	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
415	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
416	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

418	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
419	
420	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
421	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
422	
423	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
424	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
425	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
426	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
427	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
428	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
429	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
430	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
431	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
432	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
433	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
434	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
435	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
436	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
437	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
438	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
439	      definitionally.
440	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
441	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
442	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
443	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
444	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
445	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
446	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
447	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
448	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
449	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
450	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
451	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
452	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
453	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
454	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
455	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
456	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
457	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
458	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
459	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
460	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
461	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
462	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
463	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
464	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
465	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
466	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
467	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
468	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
469	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
470	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
471	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
472	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
473	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
474	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
475	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
476	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
477	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
478	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
479	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
480	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
481	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
482	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
483	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
484	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
485	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
486	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
487	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
488	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
489	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
490	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
491	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
492	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
493	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
494	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
495	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
496	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
497	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
498	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
499	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
500	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
501	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
502	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
503	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
504	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
505	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
506	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
507	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
508	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
509	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
510	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
511	    intro U
512	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
513	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
514	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
515	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
516	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
517	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
518	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
519	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
520	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
521	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
522	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
523	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm

525	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
526	
527	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
528	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
529	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
530	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
531	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
532	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
533	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
534	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
535	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
536	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
537	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
538	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
539	  intro x
540	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
541	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
542	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
543	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
544	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
545	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
546	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
547	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
548	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
549	    tensorObj_unit_iso

551	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
552	
553	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
554	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
555	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
556	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
557	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
558	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
559	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
560	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
561	
562	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
563	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
564	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
565	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
566	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
567	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
568	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
569	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
570	    IsIso φ := by
571	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
572	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
573	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
574	    intro x
575	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
576	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
577	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
578	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
579	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
580	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
581	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
582	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
583	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
584	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
585	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
586	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
587	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
588	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
589	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
590	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
591	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
592	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
593	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
594	  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
595	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
596	  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
597	    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
598	    exact this
599	  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
600	
601	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
602	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
603	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
604	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
605	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
606	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
607	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
608	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
609	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
610	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),

868	immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
869	`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`

882	lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :

898	
899	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
900	`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
901	and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
902	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.

904	This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
905	pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
906	sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
907	coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
908	`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`

969	      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))

1141	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :

1143	  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1144	      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
1145	  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙

2564	\section{Pullback-monoidality: invertibility under a general scheme morphism}
2565	\label{sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality}
2566	
2567	The relative Picard consumer (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}) re-bases its
2568	line-bundle carrier onto the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
2569	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}, and its
2570	functorial structure maps are \emph{pullback} maps of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules: the
2571	projection pullback \(\pi_T^*\) along \(C \times_S T \to T\) and the base-change
2572	pullback along \(C \times_S T' \to C \times_S T\). For these maps to descend to the
2573	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) carrier one needs that \emph{pullback preserves
2574	\(\otimes\)-invertibility} for \emph{general} morphisms of schemes --- the
2575	projection and the base-change morphism are neither open immersions nor flat. This
2576	is the target lemma \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} of the section.
2577	
2578	This is a genuinely different fact from the open-immersion compatibility
2579	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, and not a corollary of it. That lemma handles
2580	restriction along an open immersion, and it does so by routing through the
2581	\emph{lax right} adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is
2582	strong monoidal there \emph{only} because an open immersion's structure-sheaf ring
2583	map is a local \emph{isomorphism} (\(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)). A general scheme
2584	morphism has no such isomorphism, so the right-adjoint route is unavailable.
2585	
2586	\paragraph{The general comparison is descoped.} One might attempt
2587	\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} through the \emph{general} pullback--tensor
2588	comparison \(f^*(M \otimes_X N) \cong f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N\) for \emph{arbitrary}
2589	\(M, N\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}). Mathematically that isomorphism is true
2590	for every morphism --- pullback is extension of scalars and extension of scalars is
2591	strong monoidal --- but \emph{formalising the general iso is Mathlib-scale and is not
2592	needed on any project path}. The obstruction is structural: the project carries
2593	\(f^*\) \emph{abstractly}, as the left adjoint
2594	\((\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\), with no sectionwise value to which
2595	one could attach the extension-of-scalars tensorator. Building the comparison as an
2596	isomorphism by the concrete-model route would require a concrete model \(P =
2597	\mathtt{sheafification} \circ (\text{presheaf base change})\), and that model needs
2598	two ingredients absent from Mathlib at the pinned commit: a presheaf-level
2599	extension-of-scalars functor \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.extendScalars}\), and a
2600	topological inverse-image functor (a left Kan extension along
2601	\((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}).\mathrm{op}\)). The Stacks proof of the
2602	general statement is itself recorded as ``Omitted''
2603	(\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}): it is standard mathematics with no slick
2604	formalisation. We therefore \emph{descope} the general iso and prove only the
2605	invertible case, which is all the downstream consumers (relative Picard functor,
2606	Quot embedding, tangent computation, Albanese universal property) ever require.
2607	
2608	\paragraph{The route: local trivialisation of the invertible pair.} For invertible
2609	\(M, N\) the comparison map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} : f^*(M \otimes_X N) \to f^*M
2610	\otimes_Y f^*N\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) \emph{is} an isomorphism, and we
2611	prove this not in general but on the specific invertible pair, by local
2612	trivialisation. The comparison map itself exists for arbitrary \(M, N\): it is the
2613	sheaf-level transport of the presheaf-level oplax comparison
2614	(\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) through sheafification, and it sidesteps
2615	the absent inverse image because it is only a \emph{map}, not an iso. Invertibility
2616	enters only to upgrade that map to an iso: an invertible module is locally free of
2617	rank \(1\) (\cref{lem:isinvertible_implies_locallytrivial}), so \(M, N\), and hence
2618	\(M \otimes_X N\), become \(\mathcal{O}_X\) on a common affine cover; their pullbacks
2619	become \(\mathcal{O}_Y\) on the preimage cover (\(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial.pullback}\)),
2620	where \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) restricts to the canonical comparison
2621	\(\mathcal{O} \otimes \mathcal{O} \cong \mathcal{O}\), an isomorphism; and a morphism
2622	of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules that is an isomorphism after restriction to every member
2623	of a cover is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}).
2624	
2625	\paragraph{Two negative results that pin the route.} First, one might hope that since
2626	pullback is \emph{some} (op)lax monoidal functor, invertible objects are preserved for
2627	free. This is \emph{false}: global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal, yet
2628	\(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not an invertible module, so a mere
2629	oplax comparison map does \emph{not} suffice --- one genuinely has to prove the tensor
2630	comparison is an \emph{isomorphism}. This is exactly why
2631	\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} cannot be read off the presheaf-level oplax structure
2632	of \cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal} alone, and must localise.
2633	
2634	Second --- and crucially --- the local-trivialisation route is \emph{not} the shelved
2635	``invertible \(\Rightarrow\) locally \(\mathcal{O}\) \(\Rightarrow\) recover
2636	invertibility from local triviality'' mirage. That mirage is the \emph{reverse} bridge
2637	\(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsInvertible}\), the dual/tensor-inverse
2638	manufacture that the invertibility-carrier pivot (\cref{sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier}) was
2639	built to delete; it stays off-path here. Our route never recovers invertibility from
2640	local triviality: it produces the explicit witness \(f^*N\) directly and uses local
2641	triviality \emph{only} in the \emph{forward} direction
2642	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)
2643	(\cref{lem:isinvertible_implies_locallytrivial}, invertible \(\Rightarrow\) locally free
2644	of rank \(1\), which is genuinely available), and only to certify that the comparison
2645	map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is an isomorphism on that one pair.
2646	
2647	\paragraph{Guardrail.} The iso-ness of \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) must be checked
2648	\emph{on a cover} via \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, \emph{not} stalkwise.
2649	Reducing it to stalks would re-derive the varying-ring stalk-tensor commutation; the cover route avoids that
2650	entirely, because on each trivialising open all three modules are literally
2651	\(\mathcal{O}\) and the comparison is the canonical \(\mathcal{O} \otimes \mathcal{O}
2652	\cong \mathcal{O}\).
2653	
2654	\begin{lemma}
2655	[The presheaf pushforward is lax monoidal]
2656	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}
2657	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal}
2658	  Let \(\varphi\) be a morphism of \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued ring presheaves,
2659	  presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules. Then the presheaf pushforward
2660	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\varphi\) is \emph{lax monoidal}: it
2661	  carries the comparison
2662	  \[
2663	    \mu :
2664	      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
2665	        \otimes (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
2666	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2667	      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(A \otimes B),
2668	  \]
2669	  obtained as the composite of the strong-monoidal pushforward of presheaves over a
2670	  fixed ring presheaf (\(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\)) with the lax
2671	  monoidal restriction of scalars \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) along the
2672	  ring-presheaf map. This is a statement at the \emph{presheaf} level of
2673	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules; it does not require a monoidal structure on
2674	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\).
2675	\end{lemma}
2676	
2677	\begin{proof}
2678	  Restriction of scalars along a homomorphism of ring presheaves is lax monoidal --- the
2679	  comparison sends \((a \otimes b)\) to itself under the identification of the
2680	  restricted tensor product with the source tensor product --- and the pushforward of
2681	  presheaves of modules over a \emph{fixed} ring presheaf is strong monoidal. The
2682	  presheaf pushforward along \(\varphi\) factors as the composite of these two functors,
2683	  so its lax monoidal structure is the composite of the strong-monoidal structure on the
2684	  first factor with the lax structure on the second; the comparison \(\mu\) is the
2685	  whiskered composite of the two comparisons.
2686	\end{proof}
2687	
2688	\begin{lemma}
2689	[The presheaf pullback is oplax monoidal; the comparison map $\delta$]
2690	  \label{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
2691	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal}
2692	  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}
2693	  In the same setting, the presheaf pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\)
2694	  --- the left adjoint of the lax-monoidal presheaf pushforward of
2695	  \cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal} --- is \emph{oplax monoidal}. In
2696	  particular it carries the canonical comparison \emph{morphism}
2697	  \[
2698	    \delta :
2699	      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(A \otimes B)
2700	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2701	      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
2702	        \otimes (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B,
2703	  \]
2704	  produced by the doctrinal adjunction transfer
2705	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\): the oplax structure on a left
2706	  adjoint is the adjunction mate of the lax structure on its right adjoint. The
2707	  comparison \(\delta\) is at the \emph{presheaf} level, and it is only a
2708	  \emph{morphism}: upgrading \(\delta\) to an isomorphism is the remaining content, and
2709	  is exactly what the local-trivialisation argument of \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback}
2710	  supplies for an invertible pair.
2711	\end{lemma}
2712	
2713	\begin{proof}
2714	  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}
2715	  The presheaf pullback is by construction the left adjoint of the presheaf pushforward,
2716	  which is lax monoidal by \cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}. Mathlib's
2717	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\) turns the lax-monoidal structure on a
2718	  right adjoint into an oplax-monoidal structure on the left adjoint, with the oplax
2719	  comparison \(\delta\) defined as the mate of the lax comparison \(\mu\) under the
2720	  adjunction. Applying this to the pullback--pushforward adjunction yields the asserted
2721	  oplax structure and the comparison morphism \(\delta\).
2722	\end{proof}

2724	\begin{lemma}
2725	[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ for a general scheme morphism --- descoped]
2726	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}

2738	  open-immersion hypothesis is required: it holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
2739	  and all morphisms \(f\).

2772	\begin{proof}
2773	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2774	        lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
2775	  We record the would-be concrete-model proof to document where it stalls; the statement
2776	  is descoped and this argument is \emph{not} carried out in the formalisation. Read it as
2777	  an explanation of why the general iso is Mathlib-scale.
2778	
2779	  Mathematically, pullback is extension of scalars and extension of scalars is strong
2780	  monoidal, so the comparison is an isomorphism for every \(f\). The only work is to
2781	  carry this through the formalisation's \emph{abstract} pullback \(f^* =
2782	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\), which has no sectionwise value. We
2783	  do not compute \(f^*\) sectionwise; instead we equip a \emph{concrete} model of the
2784	  pullback with a strong-monoidal structure and transport it to \(f^*\) along the
2785	  uniqueness of left adjoints. This mirrors Mathlib's own construction of the
2786	  extension-of-scalars monoidal structure in
2787	  \texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean}, where the
2788	  \emph{left} adjoint's strong-monoidal structure is built explicitly (via the
2789	  base-change distributivity isomorphism) and only afterwards is the right adjoint's
2790	  lax structure derived from the adjunction --- never the reverse direction. The
2791	  argument has three steps.
2792	
2793	  \emph{Step 1 (a concrete strong-monoidal pullback \(P\)).} Build the concrete functor
2794	  \[
2795	    P \;=\; \mathtt{sheafification} \;\circ\; (\text{presheaf base change}),
2796	  \]
2797	  the presheaf-level inverse image (sectionwise extension of scalars along the
2798	  structure-sheaf ring maps of \(f\)) followed by sheafification on the small Zariski
2799	  site --- the same \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) shape already used in
2800	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. Equip \(P\) with a strong-monoidal structure. Its
2801	  tensorator is the linear-algebra strong-monoidal core
2802	  \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange}\),
2803	  \[
2804	    (M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S
2805	      \;\cong\;
2806	    (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S),
2807	  \]
2808	  an isomorphism for \emph{every} ring map \(R \to S\), with no flatness, sheafified
2809	  pointwise; this is precisely Mathlib's \((\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi).\mathtt{Monoidal}\)
2810	  lifted to sheaves. The unit comparison is the already-landed sheafification-monoidality
2811	  brick \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\). Thus \(P\) is strong monoidal with each
2812	  tensorator and the unit comparison an isomorphism, with no hypothesis on \(f\).
2813	
2814	  \emph{Step 2 (\(P\) is left adjoint to pushforward).} Exhibit an adjunction
2815	  \(P \dashv \mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforward}\,f\). This is the universal property
2816	  of sheafified extension of scalars: the presheaf base change is left adjoint to
2817	  presheaf restriction of scalars, and sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion,
2818	  so the composite \(P\) is left adjoint to the pushforward.

2821	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\)
2822	  is \emph{a} left adjoint of the pushforward. By the uniqueness of left adjoints
2823	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) --- the same device that closed
2824	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} --- there is a canonical natural isomorphism
2825	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f \cong P\). The strong-monoidal structure of
2826	  \(P\) travels along this isomorphism, so its tensorator becomes a genuine
2827	  \emph{isomorphism}
2828	  \[
2829	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2830	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2831	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
2832	  \]
2833	  natural in \(M\) and \(N\). This is the asserted comparison; the abstract pullback is
2834	  never evaluated sectionwise --- the monoidal structure is transported across the
2835	  adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism.
2836	
2837	  The locus of the difficulty is precise. A presheaf-level oplax comparison
2838	  \emph{map} \(\delta\) already exists and costs nothing: the presheaf pushforward is lax
2839	  monoidal (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}) and its left adjoint, the
2840	  presheaf pullback, is therefore oplax monoidal
2841	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}), via the bundled doctrinal adjunction
2842	  transfer \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\). The comparison \emph{map} \(\delta\) is thus free; the entire residual
2843	  difficulty is upgrading \(\delta\) to an \emph{isomorphism} for arbitrary \(M, N\).
2844	  This is what Step~3's transport along \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) would deliver --- but
2845	  only once the concrete model \(P\) of Step~1 is constructed, and \(P\) requires the
2846	  Mathlib-absent presheaf extension of scalars and topological inverse image. There is no
2847	  shortcut from the bare oplax map: an (op)lax monoidal functor does \emph{not} preserve
2848	  invertible objects for free --- global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal yet
2849	  \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not invertible --- so the iso must be
2850	  proved, not inferred from the doctrinal structure.
2851	
2852	  Because only the invertible case is needed, the substrate does not build \(P\) and does
2853	  not prove the general iso. Instead it works with the comparison map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\)
2854	  of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} (the sheaf-level transport of the presheaf \(\delta\))
2855	  and proves it is an isomorphism on a single invertible pair by local trivialisation
2856	  (\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback}), which never invokes \(P\) or the absent inverse
2857	  image.
2858	\end{proof}

2860	\begin{lemma}
2861	[The sheaf-level pullback--tensor comparison map $\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}$]
2862	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_map}
2863	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorMap}
2864	  \uses{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2865	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
2866	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
2867	  There is a canonical comparison \emph{morphism}
2868	  \[
2869	    \delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} :
2870	      (\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f).\mathrm{obj}\,(M \otimes_X N)
2871	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2872	      (\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f).\mathrm{obj}\,M
2873	        \otimes_Y (\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f).\mathrm{obj}\,N
2874	  \]
2875	  of \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules, natural in \(M\) and \(N\). It is a \emph{map only}: in
2876	  general it is not asserted to be an isomorphism.
2877	\end{lemma}
2878	
2879	\begin{proof}
2880	  \uses{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2881	  The presheaf pullback is oplax monoidal (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}),
2882	  so at the presheaf level it carries the comparison morphism
2883	  \[
2884	    \delta : (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(A \otimes B)
2885	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2886	      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
2887	        \otimes (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B,
2888	  \]
2889	  where \(\varphi\) is the ring-presheaf map presenting \(f\). One transports \(\delta\)
2890	  through sheafification to the sheaf level, using the same
2891	  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) device that supplies Steps 1--2 of
2892	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}: the abstract pullback of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules
2893	  agrees, up to canonical natural isomorphism, with the sheafification of the presheaf
2894	  pullback, and likewise the sheafified tensor product \(\otimes_Y\) is the
2895	  sheafification of the presheaf tensor. The right-hand side reconciliation of the unit
2896	  and tensor factors with their sheafified forms is supplied by the
2897	  sheafification-monoidality brick \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\). Whiskering and
2898	  composing these canonical isomorphisms with the sheafification of \(\delta\) produces
2899	  the asserted sheaf-level morphism \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\), natural in \(M, N\)
2900	  because each ingredient is. No claim of invertibility is made: \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\)
2901	  is the carrier on which the invertible-case isomorphism argument of
2902	  \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} acts.
2903	\end{proof}

2916	  the composition coherence
2917	  \[
2918	    \mathrm{upu}(h \mathbin{;} f)
2919	      \;=\;
2920	    \mathrm{upu}\,f \;\mathbin{;}\;
2921	      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathrm{upu}\,h\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
2922	      (\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{hom},
2923	  \]
2924	  where \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f : (h \mathbin{;} f)_* \cong f_* \circ h_*\) is the
2925	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism of the pushforward. Sectionwise both sides are nothing
2926	  but the functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps, so the identity holds on the
2927	  nose after the comparison of sections. This is the right-adjoint half of the unit
2928	  composition coherence, and it is the input consumed by the mate transport that produces

2934	  sections over each open. Each comparison \(\mathrm{upu}\) acts on sections as the
2935	  structure-sheaf ring homomorphism induced by the relevant morphism of schemes, and the
2936	  pseudofunctor coherence \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) acts as the identification of
2937	  \((h \mathbin{;} f)\)-pushforward sections with iterated \(f_*\,h_*\)-pushforward
2938	  sections. The composition coherence is therefore the functoriality identity
2939	  \((h \mathbin{;} f)^\sharp = h^\sharp \circ f^\sharp\) of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
2940	  which holds definitionally; the two sides agree section by section.
2941	\end{proof}

3003	  \[

3006	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,Y.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf},

3013	
3014	\begin{proof}
3015	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
3016	  This is a one-step consequence of a Mathlib instance, with no chart-chase. The
3017	  canonical comparison map is the genuine Mathlib morphism
3018	  \[
3019	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,f :
3020	      f^*\mathcal{O}_X \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
3021	  \]
3022	  the unit comparison \(\eta\) of \(f^*\); abbreviate it \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\). Mathlib
3023	  proves this map is an isomorphism whenever the comparison functor
3024	  \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}\) on opens is \(\mathtt{Final}\): the instance
3025	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}\).
3026	
3027	  The point is that the finality hypothesis holds \emph{unconditionally}, for every
3028	  morphism of schemes \(f\). The preimage functor \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}
3029	  : \mathtt{Opens}\,X \to \mathtt{Opens}\,Y\) is a frame homomorphism: it preserves
3030	  arbitrary unions and finite intersections, hence preserves all finite limits. A
3031	  functor between small categories that preserves finite limits is representably flat,
3032	  so \(\mathtt{final\_of\_representablyFlat}\) gives \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\)
3033	  with no hypothesis on \(f\) whatsoever. (There is no need to pass to affine charts:
3034	  finality is not a special feature of open immersions but a general property of
3035	  preimage-on-opens.)

3038	  directly, and \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) is an isomorphism for every \(f\). The asserted
3039	  isomorphism \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) is the bundled iso
3040	  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) of this comparison.
